profile.name: Dan Sherman
roles: Staff Sergeant, U.S. Air Force (ELINT / NSA)
Author - Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny (1997)
profile.summary: Twelve-year U.S. Air Force veteran and NSA-assigned electronic intelligence (ELINT) specialist who published 'Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny' in 1997 claiming he was secretly recruited for an NSA program that trained him to telepathically communicate with extraterrestrial entities. He claims to have received more than 75 messages from two ET contacts over approximately 10 months, including coordinates and pain metrics from human abductees, before requesting discharge in April 1997. His military background is verifiable and internally consistent; the PPD claims have no independent corroborating witnesses after nearly three decades.
education: USAF ELINT Technical School, Goodfellow AFB, Texas (c. 1990)
NSA intermediate ELINT training, Fort Meade, Maryland (1992)
early_career: Enlisted in U.S. Air Force Security Forces (Security Police) in 1982 at age 18
First Korea posting (1984) - encountered Electronic Intelligence specialty and initiated cross-training
Assigned to Offutt AFB, Nebraska (SAC Headquarters) from approximately 1990-1992

key_events.event: Enlisted in the U.S. Air Force at age 18, entering Security Forces (Security Police). During a posting to Korea in 1984, encountered an Electronic Intelligence (ELINT) specialist and began cross-training toward that field.

Completed ELINT technical school, likely at Goodfellow AFB, Texas. Subsequently assigned to Offutt Air Force Base, Nebraska, headquarters of Strategic Air Command (SAC). Deployed in support of Persian Gulf War operations.

Arrived at NSA Headquarters, Fort Meade, Maryland for intermediate ELINT training and assignment. Conventional role: analyzing electromagnetic energy for intelligence value. Held TS/SCI clearances. Public records research by Richard Geldreich Jr. found records consistent with this Offutt and Fort Meade timeline.

According to Sherman: during NSA cross-training at Fort Meade, a handler within an alleged program called Project Preserve Destiny made contact. He claims to have been informed that his mother was genetically modified in 1963 to produce a child capable of intuitive (telepathic) communications with extraterrestrial entities, and that his ELINT training was partially cover for activating that latent ability.

According to Sherman: underwent 'intuitive communicator' training involving biofeedback-style exercises on modified computer terminals at an NSA facility. The training involved mentally manipulating oscilloscope-like displays, described as unlocking a latent telepathic ability over several weeks.

According to Sherman: began active telepathic communication sessions with two entities he nicknamed 'Spock' (logical, earlier contact) and 'Bones' (primary contact over approximately 10 months). More than 75 messages were transmitted, including philosophical discussions about star cultures, acknowledgment of God, and warnings of 'substantial Earth changes.' Also received what were described as abduction data transmissions - latitudes, longitudes, and 'residual pain levels' attributed to human abductees.

According to Sherman: discontinued active PPD communications. The abduction data transmissions - pain metrics from human victims - had deeply disturbed him and were cited as the reason for disengagement. Separated from service after 12 years.

Filed for discharge from the Air Force, citing discomfort with participating in operations he believed involved harm to civilians. 'The way I obtained my discharge is not a secret' - declined to elaborate further, citing self-incrimination concerns.

Self-published 'Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny - Insider Account of Alien Contact and Government Cover-Up' (ISBN: 978-0-9660978-0-1). Stated approach: 'If I were to make up a story, it would be a lot more elaborate than this. I want to stick to what happened to me, and let everyone else conjecture upon that.' Shipped to more than 45 countries.

Appeared on Coast to Coast AM with Art Bell on November 10, 1997 - highest-profile early platform. Gave the book wide initial exposure in the UAP research community. Claimed 100+ radio and TV appearances and audiences exceeding 100,000 people across his disclosure activity.

Video interview with Project Camelot (Kerry Cassidy) in Portland, Oregon. The interview, titled 'Way Above Black,' is his most comprehensive on-camera account. Re-released by Project Camelot in March 2026 on Rumble and Odysee, indicating sustained community interest.

Researcher Noah Hradek submitted a FOIA request to the Air Force regarding 'Project Preserve Destiny.' Referred to the NSA. The NSA's response used standard Glomar language: even acknowledgment of the possible existence of such a program is classified pursuant to Executive Order 13526. Proponents cite this as indirect acknowledgment; skeptics note it is the NSA's standard reply to UAP-adjacent FOIA requests.

credibility.note: Sherman's 12-year Air Force career and NSA ELINT assignment are his primary credibility anchors - internally consistent with documented military career paths. His PPD claims have no independent corroborating witnesses after nearly 30 years. His tone is unusually restrained for UAP claimants; he consistently declines to speculate beyond his own stated experience. The 2013 Sentry Eagle structural parallel is the strongest indirect support cited by proponents. No physical evidence exists for any aspect of the PPD claims.
supporting: 12-year USAF veteran with documented ELINT specialty and NSA Fort Meade assignment - the verifiable portions of his biography are internally consistent with real Air Force career paths
Public records found by researcher Richard Geldreich Jr. show a person matching Sherman's name and birth date in the Omaha, Nebraska area during 1993-1994, consistent with an Offutt AFB posting
Unusually restrained claimant - consistently declines to speculate beyond his own stated first-person experience across nearly 30 years of public activity
Account has remained internally consistent without significant contradiction or embellishment since 1997

2013 Snowden leaks revealed the NSA's 'Sentry Eagle' architecture - a documented structure for hiding the most classified NSA operations as Exceptionally Controlled Information inside less-classified programs, paralleling what Sherman described in 1997
NSA FOIA response used Executive Order 13526 Glomar language rather than a flat denial, consistent with standard classified program procedure
contradicting: No independent corroborating witnesses have come forward in nearly 30 years - no second person has confirmed the existence of Project Preserve Destiny
No physical evidence of any kind exists for the PPD program, the intuitive communicator training, or the ET communications
The prenatal genetic modification backstory (his mother's 1960/1963 alleged visits) is unfalsifiable and follows patterns typical of constructed abduction narratives
The NSA FOIA Glomar response is the agency's standard reply to UAP-adjacent requests, not a specific acknowledgment of PPD
The Sentry Eagle structural parallel is circumstantial - it shows the architecture Sherman described is plausible, not that PPD specifically existed
Some analysts suggest Sherman may have been the subject of a psychological or biofeedback experiment, with the ET contact framing added by handlers rather than reflecting reality
No mainstream journalism or government body has investigated or commented on his account in any capacity

associated_people.name: Dr. Steven M. Greer
associated_people.relationship: Sherman's book circulated extensively in the Disclosure Project community. His testimony type - compartmentalized NSA program with ET communication component - fit the Disclosure Project framework. Both are associated with the same wave of late 1990s UAP insider disclosure efforts.

associated_people.name: Donna Hare
associated_people.relationship: Both represent mid-level government contractors and military personnel who surfaced in the late 1990s claiming compartmentalized UAP knowledge. Hare's Building 8 testimony and Sherman's PPD account both emerged from the same disclosure movement circa 1997-2001.

disclosures.title: Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny
disclosures.notes: Primary written disclosure. First-person account of USAF/NSA service and alleged PPD recruitment, intuitive communicator training, ET communications, and discharge. ISBN: 978-0-9660978-0-1. Shipped to 45+ countries.

disclosures.title: Above Black - Project Preserve Destiny Interview
disclosures.notes: Broadcast November 10, 1997. Highest-profile early platform; gave the book wide initial exposure in the UAP research community.

disclosures.title: Way Above Black
disclosures.notes: Video interview in Portland, Oregon. Most comprehensive on-camera account. Transcript PDF available at projectcamelot.org. Re-released March 2026 on Rumble and Odysee.

career_connections.relationship: Hare and Sherman represent the same category of mid-level government insiders claiming compartmentalized UAP knowledge from the same era. Both published or testified in the late 1990s disclosure window and are frequently cited together in the context of institutional insider testimony.

Stone's Army crash retrieval claims and Sherman's NSA communication program claims represent parallel compartmentalized program narratives from the same era. Both were US military enlisted personnel who claimed extraordinary UAP-adjacent assignments within the institutional structure.

Sherman's 1997 book circulated in the Disclosure Project community. Greer's framework of compartmentalized programs and insider testimony provided the institutional context in which Sherman's account was most widely received and cited.
